items.prompt: Plot the point `(3, -2)` and explain what each coordinate controls.
items.expected_evidence: explain and use circle measures including circumference, area, arc length, and sectors
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain and use circle measures including circumference, area, arc length, and sectors with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find the slope between `(1, 4)` and `(5, 12)` and interpret it as a rate of change.
items.expected_evidence: justify central-angle and inscribed-angle relationships in bounded diagrams
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates justify central-angle and inscribed-angle relationships in bounded diagrams with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Determine whether the table represents a function and justify your decision.
items.expected_evidence: compute midpoint and distance and interpret simple circle-coordinate relationships
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates compute midpoint and distance and interpret simple circle-coordinate relationships with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain how a table, graph, and equation can represent the same linear relationship.
items.expected_evidence: explain and use circle measures including circumference, area, arc length, and sectors
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to explain and use circle measures including circumference, area, arc length, and sectors.

items.prompt: Explain what slope means in both graphical and contextual terms.
items.expected_evidence: justify central-angle and inscribed-angle relationships in bounded diagrams
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to justify central-angle and inscribed-angle relationships in bounded diagrams.

items.prompt: A student reverses the coordinates and plots `(3, -2)` as `(-2, 3)`. Diagnose the mapping error. Focus on formula structure fragmentation.
items.expected_evidence: explain and use circle measures including circumference, area, arc length, and sectors
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward explain and use circle measures including circumference, area, arc length, and sectors.

items.prompt: A student says every straight-looking graph is proportional. Identify the misclassification. Focus on arc angle meaning gap.
items.expected_evidence: justify central-angle and inscribed-angle relationships in bounded diagrams
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward justify central-angle and inscribed-angle relationships in bounded diagrams.

items.prompt: A ride-share charges a fixed fee plus a cost per mile. Write a rule, make a table, and interpret the slope and intercept.
items.expected_evidence: explain and use circle measures including circumference, area, arc length, and sectors
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves explain and use circle measures including circumference, area, arc length, and sectors in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Given a graph and a verbal description, decide whether they could represent the same relationship and justify the match.
items.expected_evidence: justify central-angle and inscribed-angle relationships in bounded diagrams
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves justify central-angle and inscribed-angle relationships in bounded diagrams in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: On a delayed check, move between a rule, a table, and a graph for a linear relationship and explain the connection.
items.expected_evidence: explain and use circle measures including circumference, area, arc length, and sectors
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates explain and use circle measures including circumference, area, arc length, and sectors on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Use a prerequisite probe on congruence and similarity via transformations to see whether the learner is blocked by graph/coordinate foundations or by circles measurement and coordinate geometry itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether congruence and similarity via transformations is secure enough to support circles measurement and coordinate geometry
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in congruence and similarity via transformations from a current-node issue in circles measurement and coordinate geometry.

items.prompt: Use a prerequisite probe on coordinate plane and ordered pairs to see whether the learner is blocked by graph/coordinate foundations or by circles measurement and coordinate geometry itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether coordinate plane and ordered pairs is secure enough to support circles measurement and coordinate geometry
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in coordinate plane and ordered pairs from a current-node issue in circles measurement and coordinate geometry.
